skill.name: @azure/storage-blob (TypeScript/JavaScript)
skill.description: Skill for working with Azure Blob Storage using the @azure/storage-blob SDK in TypeScript and JavaScript. Provides guidance on blob operations, container management, and best practices for cloud storage integration.
skill.summary: TypeScript and JavaScript skill for Azure Blob Storage operations using @azure/storage-blob SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation (SKILL.md). No executable code was scanned. The skill provides guidance on using Azure Blob Storage SDK and poses no security risk. Risk score: 0/100.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers work with Azure Blob Storage by providing TypeScript and JavaScript code examples for common operations like uploading, downloading, and managing blobs and containers.
actual_capabilities: Write TypeScript code for Azure Blob Storage operations
Upload and download blobs with streaming
Implement blob leasing and concurrency handling
Handle Azure Storage errors and retries
limitations: This is a documentation and code-example skill, not a live Azure client
Does not execute Azure operations - only generates code
Requires valid Azure Storage credentials to run generated code
Does not manage Azure resource provisioning

use_cases.title: Generate blob upload code
use_cases.description: Create TypeScript code to upload files to Azure Blob Storage with proper authentication

use_cases.title: Implement container operations
use_cases.description: Generate code for creating, listing, and managing blob containers

use_cases.title: Add streaming download functionality
use_cases.description: Create code for efficient streaming downloads from Azure Blob Storage

prompt_templates.title: Basic blob upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Write TypeScript code to upload a file to Azure Blob Storage using @azure/storage-blob
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with basic blob operations

prompt_templates.title: Container creation
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I create a new container in Azure Blob Storage and set its access level?

prompt_templates.title: Streaming download
prompt_templates.prompt: Write code to download a blob using streaming in Node.js with TypeScript

prompt_templates.title: Batch operations
prompt_templates.prompt: How can I list all blobs in a container and delete them in bulk using @azure/storage-blob?

output_examples.input: Write code to upload a file to Azure Blob Storage
output_examples.output: Code example showing BlockBlobClient setup, uploadFile method, and proper authentication using Connection String

output_examples.input: How do I download a blob as text?
output_examples.output: TypeScript code using downloadToBuffer or download method with string conversion

best_practices: Use Managed Identities or Azure Key Vault for credential management in production
Implement proper error handling with Azure Storage specific exceptions
Use streaming for large files to avoid memory issues
anti_patterns: Hardcoding connection strings in source code
Loading entire large files into memory before upload

faq.question: What credentials do I need to use Azure Blob Storage?
faq.answer: You need an Azure Storage account name and either a connection string, shared key, or SAS token. For production, use Managed Identities or Azure Key Vault.

faq.question: Can this skill execute actual Azure operations?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates TypeScript code. You must run the generated code with valid Azure credentials to perform actual operations.

faq.question: How do I handle large file uploads?
faq.answer: Use the uploadFile method with BlockBlobClient for files up to 256MB, or use UploadStream for very large files to enable chunked upload.

faq.question: What is the difference between BlockBlobClient and PageBlobClient?
faq.answer: BlockBlobClient is for block blobs (unstructured data like files), while PageBlobClient is for page blobs (random access, used by Azure VM disks).

faq.question: How do I implement concurrency control for blobs?
faq.answer: Use blob leasing with LeaseClient to implement pessimistic locking, or use optimistic concurrency with ETag checks.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with JavaScript instead of TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes, the @azure/storage-blob SDK works with both TypeScript and JavaScript. Remove type annotations for JavaScript usage.
